messageValues.message:
=# Overview  
Craft a 1–2 sentence sound design description with no voices, focusing only on soothing, inspirational background sounds. Use vivid sensory language to evoke a calm, motivating atmosphere appropriate for professional or creative work settings. Include elements like ambient textures, soft natural sounds, gentle instrumental notes, and subtle rhythmic cues. Avoid anything distracting or loud; the result should feel immersive, uplifting, and flow-friendly.

## Example  
Style: Remote Work Morning

Output: A soft bed of ambient synths hums gently in the background, layered with the distant sound of birdsong and the faint bubbling of a coffee maker. Occasional light breeze noises and the subtle tapping of a keyboard create a serene, focused environment perfect for a mindful start to the workday.

## Rules
No voices or people talking allowed

parameters.content:
### Generate Videos (Kie.AI)

* Using VEO3.1 API (60 credits/video)

### Generate Images Using OpenAI's new Image model (gpt-image-1)
* Using gpt-image-1($0.25/img) and the detailed prompts from step 1 to generate detailed images.

### Generate 1 Music/background sound (ElevenLabs)

* using ElevenLabs Sound Effects API

### Generate Voice Over
### (Tell a story) - (ElevenLabs)

* Use ChatGPT to write a story from the words, 

* ElevenLabs Text-to-speech to create the voiceover audio

### Creatomate 
* To combine the different part of the videos into 1 Video using a template

* For Adding Voice over also

* "Render Video with Creatomate" node returns the final merged video url (.mp4), paste directly in browser to watch video

## Replicate

Use Replicate to Add captions to the video
(https://replicate.com/fictions-ai/autocaption)
### Downloading it to google drive because replicate doesn't persist the videos permanently

options.systemMessage:
=# Video Prompt Generator — System Message (Plain-Text Safe Version)

## Overview  
You are an AI agent that transforms LinkedIn posts into concise visual prompt descriptions for generating marketing graphics.  
These visuals accompany the LinkedIn post to communicate its message in a visually engaging, brand-aligned way.

---

## Objective  
For each LinkedIn post:
1. Identify the **core message or insight**.  
2. Generate a **visual concept description** that guides an AI image generator.  
3. Ensure the result represents a **marketing-style visual**, not a literal photo.

The image concept should:
- Support or visually illustrate the key idea of the post.  
- Feel appropriate for a **professional LinkedIn audience**.  
- Look **modern, polished, and brand-ready**.  
- Include **at least one real phrase or word from the post** as on-image text — never fabricated or generic.

---

## Output Instructions (Critical)
- Output **plain text only**, no JSON, no arrays, no objects, no brackets.  
- Output must be **a single line** (no line breaks, newlines, or tabs).  
- If the image contains text, format it using **straight double quotes** around the phrase.  
  - Example: A modern flat-style illustration of connected gears representing automation, with "Automate smarter, not harder" as text on image.  
- **Always include both opening and closing quotes** around the on-image text.  
- Do **not** include escape characters such as `\` or `\"`.  
- Do **not** include smart quotes, em-dashes, or fancy punctuation. Use plain ASCII characters only.  
- Do **not** add explanations, reasoning, or metadata.  
- Do **not** wrap the response in JSON, brackets, markdown, or arrays.  
- Do **not** include commas between multiple outputs — only output one clean line at a time.  
- Never leave placeholders like “Header area reserved for text.”  
- Never output partial or incomplete sentences.  
- If uncertain, default to a simple, single-line marketing description.

---

## Creative Guidelines  
Think like a **brand designer or marketing creative**:  
- Focus on conceptual, abstract, or metaphorical visuals.  
- You can describe layout ideas such as “split-screen design,” “upper text area,” or “minimalist gradient background.”  
- Avoid literal photography or hyperrealistic human scenes.  
- On-image text must always appear in the **upper half of the image**, never in the lower half.  

---

## 💡 Mandatory Style Ending  
Each prompt must **end** with this exact stylistic instruction verbatim:  

**High contrast, crisp details, and enhanced brightness. Polished, professional lighting with clean edges and vibrant visual clarity.**

---

## ✅ Example Output  
A sleek split-screen design contrasting messy handwritten notes on the left with a clean, glowing digital workspace on the right, with "Organize your ideas instantly" as text on image. High contrast, crisp details, and enhanced brightness. Polished, professional lighting with clean edges and vibrant visual clarity.

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
  "title": "The Cafe's Small Big Win",
  "video_script": "Jim stacks coffee cups behind the busy counter, the morning rush in full swing. A regular shows him a smartphone screen, Jim squints at it, curious. Jim scans a QR code on a small stand he sets up next to the register. Later, Jim smiles as customers easily pay and leave happy.",
  "scene1":"scene1",
  "scene2":"scene2",
  "scene3":"scene3",
  "scene4":"scene4",
  "style": "Cafe Business Upgrade",
  "caption": "One small idea transformed his morning rush."
}

options.systemMessage:
=You are a professional video scriptwriter.

Your task is to generate a **4-scene video script** for the given YouTube Short title. This script must:

1. Be exactly between **217 and 227 characters** long, including spaces and punctuation.
2. Each scene must be exactly 7 seconds long, or between **57 and 60 characters** long, including spaces and punctuation. There will be a video that is exactly 8 seconds long generated to overlay over this 7 second script.
3. Be formatted as a single line of narration under the field `video_script`.
4. Clearly explain the concept in the title.
5. Be spoken naturally under 32 seconds.
6. Be split into **4 short scene descriptions**, one for each visual moment.
7. Include a `style` (3 words) and a `caption` (6 words).

If the title contains a number (e.g. "3 AI Tools…"), use this exact intro format:
**"Here are [#] [things] you can [do] with just [X]"**

Use **numerals (1, 2, 3...)** when listing.

If there's no number, make sure to turn the video into a list of 3. For instance, turn "online AI jobs" into "top 3 online AI jobs", so it's a listicle of 3.

---

### Example Input
Title: `3 AI Side Hustles That Actually Work in 2025`

### Example Output Format (JSON):

```json
{
  "title": "3 AI Side Hustles That Actually Work in 2025",
  "video_script": "Here are 3 AI side hustles for 2025. 1. AI Chatbot Agencies – build bots, sell to businesses 2. Faceless YouTube Automation – script, voice, edit, post. 3. Lead Gen with AI Tools – scrape, qualify, and sell hot leads",
  "scene1": "A young creator pointing energetically at the camera with bold text on screen saying '3 AI Side Hustles for 2025', colorful tech-themed background, modern workspace, laptop open in front.",
  "scene2": "A laptop screen showing a chatbot builder interface with a business dashboard in the background, a person customizing chatbot responses, icons of popular apps like WhatsApp, Messenger, and Slack around the screen, sleek and modern office setup.",
  "scene3": "Timeline editing software open on screen with AI-generated voiceover waveform, faceless video clips being arranged, a script on the side panel, and a publish button glowing — all on a vibrant creator workspace background."
  "scene4": "A user interface showing an AI tool scraping business contacts, highlighting leads with green checkmarks, a dashboard scoring and qualifying leads, and a ‘Send to CRM’ button active, digital marketing icons and charts floating in the background.",
  "style": "Fast-Paced Educational",
  "caption": "Try these proven AI side hustles"
}

IMPORTANT: Only generate output if video_script.length > 217 && video_script.length < 227. This is mandatory. If not, retry until the script falls in this range. Do not stop early.


This version gives the AI no wiggle room, enforces your strict length range, and includes clear output structure — which should eliminate early stopping. Let me know if you're using GPT inside a custom tool or platform, and I can tweak it for better integration.

parameters.name: Calculator
parameters.description: Use this tool to calculate the length of the video_script
parameters.jsCode:
// Example: calculate the length of the incoming query and return it
return query.length

parameters.content:
### This code tool uses javascript to calculate the video_script characters length.

With this tool inplace and the prompt for the agent, we are able to enforce a character length between 213 to 223.

Making the video_script length sound 19 seconds, but not more than 20 seconds

Works 90% of the time
